fields.description:
## Context
A first-class `identity` entity on rebar's event machinery, modeled on the
`session_log` / `code_review` exemption pattern: gate / lifecycle / graph /
Jira-sync exempt. It holds a display name, an email, a list of provider
mappings, and a list of public keys, keyed by an opaque rebar ticket id. This
is the foundational data structure the rest of the epic builds on (event
`author_id`, authorship attestations, key-rotation eras, Jira accountId mapping).

## Why
Authorship certification and provider-neutral user mapping both need one durable
home for "who a contributor is" that is not itself a unit of dispatchable work.
Reusing the ticket substrate (like `session_log`) gives us event-sourcing,
replication, and `show`/`search` for free without polluting the dependency graph
or the Jira reconciler.

## What
Introduce `identity` as a new exempt ticket-type plus a create path and a
self-identity config pointer.

### 1. Register `identity` as an exempt ticket-type (mirror `session_log`/`code_review`)
The type must be added at EVERY site the two existing exempt types appear —
omitting one leaves a seam silently un-patched:
- `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` — add `"identity"` to the
  `$defs/ticket_type` enum (SOURCE OF TRUTH).
- `src/rebar/types.py` is **GENERATED** — regenerate with
  `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types` (do NOT hand-edit); confirm the
  `TicketType` Literal now includes `identity`.
- `src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` — add to the `_TYPES` tuple (and it is thus
  accepted by `create`); confirm the file-impact-exempt / type lists treat it
  like `session_log`.
- `src/rebar/reducer/_api.py` — add `"identity"` to `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES`
  so it is dropped from graph/health compiles (like `session_log`).
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py` — add to `EXCLUDED_SYNC_TYPES`
  so `reconcile`/`bridge_fsck` never touch it.
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py` and `.../workflow_ops.py` — add to
  the gate-exempt type tuple so plan-review/clarity/AC/quality gates always pass
  for `identity` (it is not dispatchable work).
Excluded from `ready`/`next_batch`/default `list` follows structurally from the
graph/gate exemptions above (same mechanism as `session_log`).

### 2. Create path (`rebar identity create` + library + MCP)
A single CREATE event mints an identity carrying:
- `name`: str (display name)
- `email`: str
- `mappings`: list of `{ "provider": str, "external_id": str }` — `provider` is
  an OPEN lowercase-string vocabulary (well-known values `jira`, `github`,
  `gitea`; not a closed enum, so new providers need no schema change);
  `external_id` is the provider's opaque stable id (NEVER an email).
- `keys`: list of `{ "algorithm": str, "public_key": str }` — `algorithm` matches
  the foundation attest scheme vocabulary (e.g. `ssh-ed25519`); `public_key` is
  the OpenSSH authorized-keys public-key line (`ssh-ed25519 AAAA...`).
`show` renders name/email/mappings/keys. (The provider-vocabulary and key-format
decisions are recorded as an ADR in sibling task 4b5b — this ticket adopts the
defaults above.)

### 3. Self-identity config pointer (`.rebar/current_identity`)
- **Path**: `.rebar/current_identity` (the whole `.rebar/` dir is already
  git-ignored via `.gitignore:11`, so no gitignore edit is needed — mirrors
  `.rebar/current_session_log`, see `src/rebar/_commands/session_log.py`).
- **Format**: JSON object `{"identity_id": "<ticket_id>"}` (single key; extra
  keys tolerated/ignored for forward-compat).
- **Resolution (`resolve_current_identity()` returns `Optional[str]` id)**:
  1. If the pointer file exists and names an existing `identity` ticket → use it.
  2. Else default by matching `git config user.email` (exact, case-insensitive)
     against identity tickets' `email`.
- **Fallbacks (all return `None` — identity is opt-in, so unauthenticated is a
  valid state, never an error)**: git email unset/unconfigured → `None`; zero
  identities match → `None`; two-or-more identities match → `None` and a WARNING
  naming the ambiguity; pointer names a deleted/absent identity → fall through to
  the git-email match, else `None`.

## Scope
In scope: the type registration, the create path (CLI/lib/MCP), `show`
rendering, the config pointer + resolver with its fallbacks. Out of scope:
stamping events with `author_id` (4e9d), authorship signing (bde5), key rotation
(e165), Jira mapping (264f/2f13), enforcement (3183).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A new `identity` ticket-type is registered at all seven sites above and is
  exempt from lifecycle/gates/graph/Jira-sync (mirrors `session_log`); a test
  asserts it is excluded from `ready`/`next_batch`/default `list` and passes the
  per-ticket gates trivially.
- [ ] A create path (`rebar identity create` + library `rebar.create_identity` +
  MCP) mints an identity in one CREATE carrying name, email, mappings, keys; a
  test round-trips create -> `show` and asserts all four fields render.
- [ ] `resolve_current_identity()` reads `.rebar/current_identity`
  (`{"identity_id": ...}`) when present and otherwise matches `git config
  user.email` case-insensitively; a test asserts: (a) pointer hit, (b) git-email
  default hit, (c) each fallback (no email / zero match / multiple match /
  dangling pointer) returns `None` without raising.

## file_impact
- `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` (ticket_type enum)
- `src/rebar/types.py` (regenerated)
- `src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` (_TYPES + create)
- `src/rebar/reducer/_api.py` (_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py` (EXCLUDED_SYNC_TYPES)
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py` + `workflow_ops.py` (gate-exempt)
- new identity module (create path + resolver) + CLI/lib/MCP wiring
